title: Checkpoint failed due to not able to add socket to tar
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Cannot checkpoint and export

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run sql in container

2. podman container checkpoint --tcp-established -l --export=/tmp/1.tar.gz


**Describe the results you received:**

ERRO[0017] Can't add file /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/f783017883a46a071165d988f6e54c31ff094a8cb018d6bd2c0fd1519b56a7a6/diff/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock to tar: archive/tar: sockets not supported

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successful checkpoint and export

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.7.0-rc1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.1
  podman version: 1.7.0-rc1
host:

  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.8, commit: d35534bc4b9bc722bf1ab6f4e828fcaa6bee236a-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  MemFree: 1970593792
  MemTotal: 16273141760
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 993243136
  SwapTotal: 993243136
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: hostu18
  kernel: 4.15.0-66-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 7h 59m 6.34s (Approximately 0.29 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 3
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: {}
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes

```


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
on Aliyun ecs

comments.body: @zhuguoliang Not sure this can be fixed. But you can trying starting the container and telling Podman to mount a tmpfs at /run? Something like `podman run --tmpfs /run`.
It worked!    But why it works?

> It worked!

Perfect!

> But why it works?

During checkpointing `tmpfs` are handled by CRIU and are then part of the checkpoint. Directories with volatile data are, for the migration use-case, best mounted as `tmpfs` during container start.

In the end I am not sure why it fails. CRIU also uses `tar` to transfer the `tmpfs` content. CRIU, however, uses the `tar` binary. I guess the GO tar implementation which is part of Podman is just missing the feature to correctly add sockets to an archive.

The error occurs because [`archive/tar`](https://github.com/golang/go/blob/50bd1c4d4eb4fac8ddeb5f063c099daccfb71b26/src/archive/tar/common.go#L658) fails when a file has ModeSocket. This change was introduced with Go 1.9 (commit: https://github.com/golang/go/commit/66b5a2f3f0b1d94f69763114a85a977f5bc0277a).
